2603.15692.title: BadLLM-TG: A Backdoor Defender powered by LLM Trigger Generator
2603.15692.paper_summary: Backdoor attacks compromise model reliability by using triggers to manipulate outputs. Trigger inversion can accurately locate these triggers via a generator and is therefore critical for backdoor defense. However, the discrete nature of text prevents existing noise-based trigger generator from being applied to nature language processing (NLP). To overcome the limitations, we employ the rich knowledge embedded in large language models (LLMs) and propose a Backdoor defender powered by LLM Trigger Generator, termed BadLLM-TG. It is optimized through prompt-driven reinforcement learning, using the victim model's feedback loss as the reward signal. The generated triggers are then employed to mitigate the backdoor via adversarial training. Experiments show that our method reduces the attack success rate by 76.2\% on average, outperforming the second-best defender by 13.7.
2603.15692.paper_summary_zh: 後門攻擊通過使用觸發器來操縱輸出，從而損害模型的可靠性。觸發器反轉可以通過生成器準確定位這些觸發器，因此對於後門防禦至關重要。然而，文本的離散性使得現有的基於噪聲的觸發器生成器無法應用於自然語言處理（NLP）。為了克服這些限制，我們利用大型語言模型（LLMs）中嵌入的豐富知識，提出了一種由LLM觸發器生成器驅動的後門防禦器，稱為BadLLM-TG。它通過基於提示的強化學習進行優化，使用受害模型的反饋損失作為獎勵信號。然後生成的觸發器被用來通過對抗訓練來減輕後門影響。實驗表明，我們的方法平均將攻擊成功率降低了76.2\%，超過第二最佳防禦者13.7。
2603.15692.author: Ruyi Zhang et.al.
2603.15692.authors: Ruyi Zhang, Heng Gao, Songlei Jian, Yusong Tan, Haifang Zhou
